MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Plaintiff Jay Epps's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment, filed December 20, 2024, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted and judgment shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Order and Consent Judgment are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Clifford A. Chanler, Esq., of Chanler, LLC, (475) 277-2932, for Plaintiff.

Willis Wagner, Esq., of Arnold & Porter Kaye Scholer LLP, for Defendant.

Susan S. Fiering, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, (510) 622-2270, for Office of the California Attorney General.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar May-20-2024 Continued To Aug-14-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SHEIN US SERVICES, LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. The Wagner declaration does not state that a pre-filing meet-and-confer was held "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" or that plaintiff provided legal support for his positions in the meet-and-confer. The new response date is May 31, but this is a dispute that should be resolved by counsel.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
